subject: UX & Research
title: Why Redesigns Fail
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Why Redesigns Fail.

hook.text: Digg, Snapchat, and Tumblr all lost millions of users after major redesigns. A pattern emerges from the wreckage.

buildup.text: Most failed redesigns share one trait: they optimized for new users while ignoring the existing community.

discovery.text: Existing users have muscle memory, learned paths, and emotional attachment to the current design.

twist.text: Gmail's redesigns succeed because they're incremental. Small changes over months, not a total overhaul on one Tuesday.

climax.text: Ship big changes in small steps. Let users adjust gradually rather than shocking them with everything at once.

punchline.text: Incremental change wins. Big-bang redesigns shock and scatter users.

quiz.question: What do most failed redesigns have in common?
options: They optimized for new users while ignoring existing users' habits and mental models
They didn't change enough to make a difference
